name: Cuenca – circular through the Castillo district and the Huécar river gorge
name_original: Cuenca – circular por el barrio del Castillo y el barranco del río Huécar
region_intro: Hiking around Serrania Alta, a district within Spain's Serranía de Cuenca, offers diverse terrain characterized by distinctive karst landscapes. The region features elevated plateaus, deep canyons, and sickle-shaped gorges carved by rivers like the Júcar, Cuervo, and Escabas. Extensive pine forests cover the mountainous areas, complemented by unique geological depressions such as "torcas" and "hoyas." This varied topography provides a range of hiking experiences across its natural features. Best hiking trails around Serrania Alta The most popular hiking route is Enchanted City Trail, a 1.6 miles (2.5 km) trail that takes 40 minutes to complete. This easy path explores the surreal limestone formations of Ciudad Encantada. Another top favourite among local hikers is Tour of Los Callejones de Las Majadas – Natural Park of the Serranía de Cuenca, an easy 2.3 miles (3.7 km) path. It leads through unique rock formations shaped by erosion, similar to the Enchanted City. Local hikers also love the Hoz del Jucar and San Julian Trail, a 4.4 miles (7.1 km) trail leading through the Júcar River canyon, often completed in about 1 hour 59 minutes. Hiking around Serrania Alta is defined by dramatic karst relief, deep river gorges, and extensive pine forests. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy strolls to more demanding excursions. The routes in Serrania Alta are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 3000 hikers have used komoot to explore Serrania Alta's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Serrania Alta?
A: Serrania Alta offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 180 routes available. These range from easy strolls to more demanding excursions, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, Serrania Alta has many easy trails. Approximately 60 routes are classified as easy. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010714930/enchanted-city-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enchanted City Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a short, easy path exploring unique rock formations. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013912609/tour-of-los-callejones-de-las-majadas-natural-park-of-the-serrania-de-cuenca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Los Callejones de Las Majadas&lt;/a&gt;, which also features fascinating geological formations.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Serrania Alta?
A: Yes, circular routes are available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010700038/parque-natural-de-la-serrania-de-cuenca-circular-to-laguna-de-una" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca – circular to Laguna de Uña&lt;/a&gt; is an easy circular trail. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013889646/las-torcas-de-cuenca-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Torcas de Cuenca – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which allows you to explore geological depressions.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I visit while hiking in Serrania Alta?
A: The region is rich in natural wonders. You can explore the surreal limestone formations at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5393773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enchanted City&lt;/a&gt;, or witness the impressive waterfalls at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4740317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Cuervo River&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013332001/cascada-del-molino-de-la-chorrera-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Molino de la Chorrera Trail&lt;/a&gt; leads to a picturesque 30-meter waterfall. Don't miss the breathtaking panoramic views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3790692" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puntal de la Tola Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6268434" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estrecho del Horcajo Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there more challenging or longer hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for those seeking a greater challenge, Serrania Alta offers about 12 difficult routes and 108 moderate routes. Trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013685249/el-cucurucho-and-la-hocecilla-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cucurucho and La Hocecilla Trail&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013397511/dehesa-de-los-olmos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa de los Olmos Trail&lt;/a&gt; provide longer distances and more significant elevation changes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Serrania Alta?
A: The changing colors of the forests, especially in autumn, offer a visually spectacular experience. Spring and early summer also provide pleasant temperatures and lush green environments. While winter hiking is possible, be prepared for colder conditions and potential snow at higher altitudes.

Q: What do other hikers appreciate most about Serrania Alta?
A: The komoot community highly rates the hiking experience in Serrania Alta, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 500 reviews. Hikers often praise the dramatic karst relief, the deep river gorges, and the extensive pine forests, as well as the unique geological formations like those found in the Enchanted City.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while hiking in Serrania Alta?
A: Serrania Alta is part of a protected area known for its diverse flora and fauna. Hikers might spot various wildlife, including vultures, raptors, deer, wild boars, and mouflons, especially in the quieter, less populated areas.

Q: Are hiking trails in Serrania Alta dog-friendly?
A: While specific regulations can vary, generally, dogs are permitted on trails in natural parks like Serrania Alta, provided they are kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Always check local signage for any specific restrictions.

Q: Are there options for multi-day hikes or overnight stays in the region?
A: For those undertaking longer excursions, the region offers basic accommodations such as the Refuge de la Halconera, providing a place to rest and spend the night. This allows for more extensive exploration of the area's diverse landscapes.

Q: How accessible are the trailheads by public transport or car?
A: Due to the region's low population density, accessing trailheads by car is generally the most convenient option. Parking facilities are typically available near popular starting points for trails like the Enchanted City. Public transport options may be limited, so planning your journey in advance is advisable.

Q: Can I find trails that follow rivers or offer river views?
A: Absolutely. The region is characterized by rivers like the Júcar, Cuervo, and Escabas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013860122/hoz-del-jucar-and-san-julian-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz del Jucar and San Julian Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, leads through the Júcar River canyon, offering scenic river views and insights into the area's ancient geology.
tips: Remains of the wall and an arch of what was a fortress of Arab origin located at the top of the city.
Good views and ruin near the Mirador Barrio del Castillo.
